questions.text: Propose which one of the following reactions is more likely to occur through SN1 mechanism?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: This reaction is more likely to proceed through SN1 mechanism because generally, tertiary alkyl halides are more reactive through this mechanism. They form tertiary carbocation which is most stable hence tertiary alkyl halide is more reactive. The order of reactivity for SN1 reaction is as follows:Tertiary R-X> Secondary R-X> Primary R-XSN1 reaction is also favoured by polar solvent.

questions.text: An aldehyde when strongly heated with Fehling’s reagent gives red precipitate. What property of an aldehyde is observed in this reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Aliphatic aldehydes form a brick-red precipitate with Fehling’s solution. To an aldehyde solution, add Fehling’s solution and boil. A brick red precipitate of cuprous oxide is formed. Ketones do not give this test. The aldehyde group of sugar is oxidised by complexed copper ions to form acid. The red copper (I) oxide then precipitates, which is an indicator for the redox reaction.

questions.text: Predict which one of the following compound is a monomer of an addition polymer.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Vinyl chloride forms condensation polymer (polyvinyl chloride).

questions.explanation: Formaldehyde polymerises into metaformaldehyde. Both formaldehyde and acetaldehyde polymerize in the presence of dil. H2SO4 to give metaformaldehyde and paraldehyde respectively.

questions.text: Identify which one of the following has the highest boiling point
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Melting and boiling points of alcohols are higher than corresponding alkanes. Methyl alcohol and ethyl alcohol are liquids while methane and ethane are gases. This is also due to hydrogen bonding which is present in alcohols but absent in alkanes.Generally, boiling point of primary alcohols is greater than that of secondary alcohol which is greater than that of tertiary alcohols.The boiling point also increases with increasing number of carbon atoms hence 1-butanol has highest boiling point.

questions.text: Identify the product Y in the following scheme C6H6+CH3Cl AlCl3 X KMnO4 / H2SO4 Y
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The introduction of an alkyl group in the benzene ring in the presence of an alkyl halide and a catalyst AICI3 is called Friedel-Crafts alkylation or Alkylation. In the first step alkyl group is added to benzene resulting in the formation of toluene, hence X is toluene.In the second step toluene is oxidised in the presence of KMnO4 which is called 'side chain oxidation'. It results in formation of benzoic acid. So, Y is benzoic acid.

questions.text: Predict the co-ordination number in [Pt (OH)2 (NH3)4] SO4.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The number of lone pair of electrons provided by the ligands to the central metal atom or ion is called the coordination number of the central metal atom or ion.In this complex, both NH3 and OH are monodentate ligands (meaning they donate single electron pair) and there are 2 OH groups attached to central atom and 4 amino (NH3) groups attached so coordination number is 2+4=6.

questions.text: Identify a mixture of two organic solvents that are used in nail polish remover.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ketonic group is present in camphor and methone which is used in cosmetics and fragrances.Hence, acetone is used in nail polish remover along with ethyl acetate.

questions.text: RNA contains four different nitrogenous bases EXCEPT.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: DNA and RNA differ in three ways. 1. The sugar in RNA is ribose while the sugar in DNA is 2-deoxyribose. 2. Four different bases are found in DNA cytosine (C), thymine (T), adenine (A) and guanine (G). In RNA, .thymine does not occur and its place is taken by uracil (U). 3. DNA is nearly always double stranded, while RNA is usually single stranded.

questions.text: When ozone is treated with alkene, aldehyde and ketone are produced, identify which one of the following will produce two moles of butanone.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The ozonolysis of 3,4-dimethyl-3-hexene involves breaking the carbon-carbon double bond, resulting in the formation of carbonyl compounds. The specific carbonyl compounds produced depend on the structure of the initial alkene. In this case, 3,4-dimethyl-3-hexene will produce two similar carbonyl compounds i.e 2 moles of butanone.CH3CH2-C(CH3)=C(CH3)-CH2CH3 + O3 => unstable ozonide => 2 CH3COCH2CH3

questions.text: Cyclopropane is an example of
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The homocyclic compounds which contain a ring of three or more carbon atoms and resembling aliphatic compounds are called alicyclic compounds. The saturated alicyclic hydrocarbons have the general formula CnH2n. Cyclopropane is an alicyclic compound.

questions.text: Identify an element with higher ionization energy
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The metallic character and first ionization energy of an element are inversely related.
Ionization energy is inversely related to atomic size.
A reducing agent supplies electrons to the substance being reduced. Hence, reducing agent is expected to have low ionization enthalpy.

questions.explanation: The factors upon which the ionization energy of an atom mainly depends are magnitude of nuclear charge, size of the atom, and the “shielding effect”.Ionization energy is inversely related to metallic character.Ionization energy is inversely related to atomic size.Ionization energy is inversely related to strength of reducing agent.Ionization energy is inversely related to electropositive character and directly to electronegative character.The tendency of an element to lose the electrons to form the positive ions is called the electropositive character. It is also called the metal character. The elements which have very low ionization energies have a higher tendency to lose electrons and therefore they are electropositive or metallic in their behaviour.

questions.text: Quote the wave length range of IR region
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Here are the approximate wavelength ranges for different portions of the infrared region:Near-infrared (NIR): 0.75 μm to 1.5 μmShort-wavelength infrared (SWIR): 1.5 μm to 3 μmMid-wavelength infrared (MWIR): 3 μm to 8 μmLong-wavelength infrared (LWIR): 8 μm to 15 μmFar-infrared (FIR): 15 μm to 1000 μm (or 1 mm)

questions.text: Predict which one of the following metal hydroxide is least soluble in water?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The solubility of alkaline earth metal hydroxides in water increases down the group. Be(OH)2 is quite insoluble. Mg(OH)2 is sparingly soluble while Ba (OH)2 is more soluble. This increase in solubility is due to low lattice energy of hydroxides which is, in turn, due to higher ionic size. Since Ca(OH2) has smaller size and higher lattice energy so it is least soluble.

questions.text: Name which one of the following gas is not pollutant?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Among sulfur dioxide (SO2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and nitrogen dioxide (NO2), carbon dioxide (CO2) is not typically considered an air pollutant in the same sense as the others.Carbon dioxide is a naturally occurring gas in the Earth's atmosphere and is essential for life on Earth. It is produced through natural processes such as respiration and volcanic eruptions, as well as human activities such as combustion of fossil fuels and deforestation. While carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas and contributes to climate change when present in excessive amounts, it is not directly toxic to humans at typical ambient concentrations.On the other hand, sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide, and nitrogen dioxide are all considered air pollutants due to their harmful effects on human health and the environment.

questions.text: Predict the color change when a base is added into potassium dichromate solution
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Potassium dichromate is a powerful oxidizing agent, and its color changes depending on its oxidation state. In an acidic solution, it exists as dichromate ion Cr2O72- which is orange in color. However, when a base is added to the solution, the dichromate ion reacts to form the chromate ion CrO42- which is yellow in color.

questions.text: Identify carboxylic acid which is present in Vinegar
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The common names of carboxylic acids were derived from the source from which they are isolated. The irritation caused by an ant bite is due to formic acid (Latin word formica, ant). It was first isolated by the distillation of red ants. Similarly acetic acid was first isolated from vinegar and butyric acid was named after butyrum means butter.

questions.text: Prioritize the highest acidity of carboxylic acid in the following
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids are most acidic organic compounds. Resonance stabilization of carboxylate ion allows the negative charge to be delocalised among the two electronegative oxygen atoms. Addition of adjacent electron withdrawing substituents increases acidic character. Hence substituted chloroethanoic acid will be more acidic than simple ethanoic acid and others.Acidic character can also be hinted from the pKa values as follows:ethanoic acid pKa= 4.7propanoic acid pKa= 4.9chloroethanoic acid pKa= 2.92-meethyl propanoic acid pKa= 4.9
